title: Can't build RadAnalytics image using `buildah bud`
body:
**Description**

This is a dup of https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/518

buildah bud stops building this image when it hits the RUN yum steps. This Dockerfile is using a centos base image.

Error status is 139


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.  git clone  https://github.com/radanalyticsio/oshinko-s2i.git
2. sudo su
3. cd /oshinko-s2i/java-build
4. buildah bud -t myradimage .

Dockerfile is:
https://github.com/radanalyticsio/oshinko-s2i/blob/master/java-build/Dockerfile

**Describe the results you received:**
```
STEP 1: FROM fabric8/s2i-java:latest
STEP 2: ENV JBOSS_IMAGE_NAME="radanalyticsio/radanalytics-java-spark"     JBOSS_IMAGE_VERSION="1.0"     RADANALYTICS_JAVA_SPARK="1.0"     PATH="/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/opt/spark/bin"     SPARK_HOME="/opt/spark"     APP_ROOT="/opt/app-root" 
STEP 3: LABEL name="$JBOSS_IMAGE_NAME"       version="$JBOSS_IMAGE_VERSION"       architecture="x86_64"       com.redhat.component="radanalyticsio-radanalytics-java-spark-docker"       io.k8s.description="Platform for building a radanalytics java spark app"       io.k8s.display-name="radanalytics java_spark"       io.openshift.expose-services="8080:http"       io.openshift.s2i.scripts-url="image:///usr/local/s2i"       io.openshift.tags="builder,radanalytics,java_spark"       io.radanalytics.sparkdistro="https://archive.apache.org/dist/spark/spark-2.2.0/spark-2.2.0-bin-hadoop2.7.tgz"       org.concrt.version="1.4.0"
STEP 4: USER root
STEP 5: RUN yum install -y  epel-release tar wget     && yum clean all &&     rpm -q  epel-release tar wget
error building: error building at step {Env:[APP_ROOT=/opt/app-root SPARK_HOME=/opt/spark PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/opt/spark/bin RADANALYTICS_JAVA_SPARK=1.0 JBOSS_IMAGE_VERSION=1.0 JBOSS_IMAGE_NAME=radanalyticsio/radanalytics-java-spark PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/local/s2i JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java JOLOKIA_VERSION=1.4.0 PROMETHEUS_JMX_EXPORTER_VERSION=0.1.0 AB_JOLOKIA_PASSWORD_RANDOM=true AB_JOLOKIA_AUTH_OPENSHIFT=true JAVA_DATA_DIR=/deployments/data       JBOSS_IMAGE_NAME=radanalyticsio/radanalytics-java-spark JBOSS_IMAGE_VERSION=1.0 RADANALYTICS_JAVA_SPARK=1.0 PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/opt/spark/bin SPARK_HOME=/opt/spark APP_ROOT=/opt/app-root] Command:run Args:[yum install -y  epel-release tar wget     && yum clean all &&     rpm -q  epel-release tar wget] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN yum install -y  epel-release tar wget     && yum clean all &&     rpm -q  epel-release tar wget Original:RUN yum install -y  epel-release tar wget     && yum clean all &&     rpm -q  epel-release tar wget}: exit status 139
```
***Last step in debug
```
DEBU[0000] error running runc [/bin/sh -c yum install -y  epel-release tar wget     && yum clean all &&     rpm -q  epel-release tar wget]: exit status 139 
ERRO[0000] error building: error building at step {Env:[APP_ROOT=/opt/app-root SPARK_HOME=/opt/spark PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/opt/spark/bin RADANALYTICS_JAVA_SPARK=1.0 JBOSS_IMAGE_VERSION=1.0 JBOSS_IMAGE_NAME=radanalyticsio/radanalytics-java-spark PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/local/s2i JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java JOLOKIA_VERSION=1.4.0 PROMETHEUS_JMX_EXPORTER_VERSION=0.1.0 AB_JOLOKIA_PASSWORD_RANDOM=true AB_JOLOKIA_AUTH_OPENSHIFT=true JAVA_DATA_DIR=/deployments/data       JBOSS_IMAGE_NAME=radanalyticsio/radanalytics-java-spark JBOSS_IMAGE_VERSION=1.0 RADANALYTICS_JAVA_SPARK=1.0 PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/opt/spark/bin SPARK_HOME=/opt/spark APP_ROOT=/opt/app-root] Command:run Args:[yum install -y  epel-release tar wget     && yum clean all &&     rpm -q  epel-release tar wget] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN yum install -y  epel-release tar wget     && yum clean all &&     rpm -q  epel-release tar wget Original:RUN yum install -y  epel-release tar wget     && yum clean all &&     rpm -q  epel-release tar wget}: exit status 139 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
A successful build.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**


```
buildah-0.15-1.gitd1330a5.fc27.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 0.15 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux xxxx.xxxx.redhat.com 4.15.7-300.fc27.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Feb 28 17:53:39 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
 - storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
 - that share the containers/storage libraries
 - See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

 - The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

 - Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

 - Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

 - Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
 - AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
 - Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

 - Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
 - certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

 - OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body:
Hm. On a brand spankin' new Fedora Cloud Base VM (inside OpenStack), I ran:

```
$ sudo -s
# yum -y install git buildah
# yum -y update && reboot
```

I probably should have run that update before installing git and buildah. Bad habit, not sure if it matters. Then I ran:

```
$ git clone https://github.com/radanalyticsio/oshinko-s2i.git
$ sudo -s
# cd /oshinko-s2i/java-build
# buildah bud -t myradimage .
```

It seemed to work as expected - I did not get the errors @ipbabble is seeing and the image was successfully created.

```
[root@radanalytics-buildah ~]# buildah images
IMAGE ID             IMAGE NAME                                               CREATED AT             SIZE
cf1a9c522393         docker.io/fabric8/s2i-java:latest                        Mar 18, 2018 08:01     527.8 MB
aa6fafc55e51         docker.io/library/myradimage:latest                      Mar 19, 2018 23:33     894.1 MB
```

Here is more info on my environment:

```
# buildah --version
buildah version 0.15 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
# rpm -q buildah
buildah-0.15-1.gitd1330a5.fc27.x86_64
# cat /etc/fedora-release
Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven)
# uname -a
Linux radanalytics-buildah.novalocal 4.15.9-300.fc27.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Mar 12 17:07:55 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

I noticed that the kernel version is different from @ipbabble's but the version of buildah is the same.

Ack @rossturk  I think this laptop might be hosed due to some mixes of upstream build install and dnf install. I'm thinking I need to clean things up. Might also be why I'm having issues with the system containers while @giuseppe is not having issues.  I smell a time wasting environment.

Confirmed @rossturk  thanks for checking this and helping  the engineers not waste time. 
I uninstalled everything and reinstalled Buildah. Still had a problem. However the following saved the day (just like /var/lib/docker) rm -rf /var/lib/containers.
